codeReview.summary: Solid implementation that covers all 9 requirements from the feature description. shadcn/ui is properly configured with New York style and 18 core primitives installed (sonner covers both toast/toaster). ViewContextProvider, RootProviders, DashboardShell, Header, dashboard layout, api-client, and query-client are all implemented correctly. Tests cover ViewContext (3 tests) and DashboardShell role-aware rendering (7 tests) as specified. Code follows project conventions (Bun, @/ imports, Tailwind CSS, 'use client' only where needed). The auth gate in dashboard/layout.tsx correctly redirects to /sign-in when unauthenticated. Dark mode via next-themes is properly integrated with suppressHydrationWarning on the html tag.

issues.description: apiClient always calls res.json() on success responses. DELETE endpoints that return 204 No Content would cause a JSON parse error. Consider checking res.status === 204 or Content-Length before parsing, or using res.json().catch(() => undefined) as a fallback.
Content-Type: application/json header is set for all requests including GET. While not harmful, it's unconventional and could theoretically cause issues with strict servers or caching proxies.

getSessionCookie() uses dynamic import for next/headers (await import('next/headers')). Since this is already a React Server Component, next/headers can be imported statically at the top of the file. The dynamic import adds unnecessary overhead.

handleSignOut() has no error handling — if the POST to /api/auth/sign-out fails, the user is still redirected to /sign-in, potentially leaving a stale session cookie. Consider wrapping in try/catch or at least logging the error.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used sonner (via shadcn/ui's sonner integration) instead of the older shadcn/ui toast component. The feature description lists 'toast, toaster' as separate primitives, but the actual implementation installs sonner.tsx which is the current shadcn/ui recommended approach. This is the correct modern pattern but future workers should know that 'toast' in this project means sonner, not the legacy shadcn/ui toast.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/ui/sonner.tsx created; no src/components/ui/toast.tsx or toaster.tsx exist. RootProviders.tsx imports { Toaster } from '@/components/ui/sonner'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The api-client.ts module establishes a convention for client-side API calls: all requests go through the `api` object (api.get, api.post, api.patch, api.delete) with automatic JSON parsing, error envelope parsing into ApiClientError, and credentials: include. This should be documented as the standard pattern for client-side data fetching.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/api-client.ts — exports `api` object and `ApiClientError` class. Used implicitly by all future TanStack Query hooks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted in skillFeedback that the frontend-worker skill step 4 (write tests FIRST / red phase) is impractical for foundation setup work like installing packages and creating layout components. The skill's TDD requirement makes more sense for feature-level components with logic, not scaffolding tasks. The deviation was justified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill step 4 says write tests first (red) but for foundation setup work like installing packages and creating layout components it is more practical to create components and tests together'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker configured Tailwind v4 with shadcn/ui using oklch colors and the @custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *)) pattern plus @plugin 'tailwindcss-animate'. This is the correct Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui setup pattern but is not documented in the library. Future workers extending the theme or adding new CSS variables should reference globals.css for the established pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/globals.css — 165 lines of CSS variable definitions using oklch color space, @theme inline block, and @custom-variant for dark mode. This pattern differs from Tailwind v3 conventions.

summary: The ui-foundation feature is well-implemented and passes review. All 9 requirements from the feature description are fulfilled: shadcn/ui with New York style and 18 primitives, ViewContextProvider with useViewContext hook, RootProviders wrapping theme/query/toast providers, root layout with Inter font, DashboardShell with role-aware sidebar, Header with owner/viewer variants, dashboard layout with auth gate, api-client typed fetch wrapper, and query-client config. Tests cover ViewContext (3 tests) and DashboardShell (7 tests) as specified. Four non-blocking issues identified: api-client may fail on 204 responses, unnecessary Content-Type on GETs, dynamic import of next/headers in RSC, and missing error handling in sign-out. None are blocking for the MVP.
